text: Document Parsing & Document Management Layer Notes These notes cover the complete learning journey for building a production-style document ingestion and parsing pipeline for an AI-powered RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) system. The notes include architecture, theory, backend APIs, OCR, PDF parsing, metadata management, and FastAPI integration. 1

. 1. What is Document Parsing? Document parsing is the process of converting uploaded files such as PDFs, scanned notes, DOCX files, and images into structured machine-readable text. Why parsing is important: - LLMs cannot directly understand raw PDFs. - Retrieval quality depends on parsed text quality. - OCR is needed for scanned documents. - Good parsing reduces hallucinations in RAG systems. Parsing pipeline: Upload PDF -> Parsing -> OCR -> Cleaning -> Chunking -> Embeddings -> Vector DB 2

. High Level Architecture Production-style architecture: Frontend ↓ Upload API ↓ Storage Layer ↓ Parser Service ↓ OCR Engine ↓ Cleaning Layer ↓ Chunking Service ↓ Embedding Service ↓ Vector Database Key idea: Parsing is one of the most important layers in the entire RAG architecture.

3. Libraries Used Libraries installed: pip install fastapi pip install uvicorn pip install pymupdf pip install pytesseract pip install pillow pip install python-multipart Purpose of each library: FastAPI -> Backend API framework Uvicorn -> ASGI server PyMuPDF (fitz) -> PDF parsing pytesseract -> OCR Pillow -> Image processing python-multipart -> File upload handling 4

. Recommended Folder Structure Exam_Intelligence/ I III app/ I III __init__.py I III main.py I I I III api/ I I III __init__.py I I III upload.py I I I III services/ I I III __init__.py I I III parser.py I III uploads/ III parsed/ III documents.json III requirements.txt Important: __init__.py files are required so Python recognizes folders as packages. 5. FastAPI Main Application main.py initializes FastAPI and connects routes. Code Template:

from fastapi import FastAPI from app.api.upload import router as upload_router app = FastAPI() app.include_router(upload_router) @app.get("/") def home(): return { "message": "Server Running" } 6. Upload API Responsibilities The upload API is responsible for: - Receiving PDF files - Validating file type - Saving uploaded documents - Calling parser service - Returning parsed content - Storing metadata This follows modular backend architecture

. from fastapi import APIRouter, UploadFile, File import shutil import os from app.services.parser import PDFParser router = APIRouter( prefix="/api", tags=["Upload"] ) UPLOAD_DIR = "uploads" os.makedirs(UPLOAD_DIR, exist_ok=True) @router.post("/upload") async def upload_pdf(file: UploadFile = File(.)): file_path = os.path.join(UPLOAD_DIR, file.filename) with open(file_path, "wb") as buffer: shutil.copyfileobj(file.file, buffer) parser = PDFParser(file_path) parsed_data = parser.parse() return {
buffer) parser = PDFParser(file_path) parsed_data = parser.parse() return { "message": "PDF uploaded and parsed successfully", "pages": parsed_data } 7
. Parser Service The parser service is the core intelligence layer for ingestion.

Responsibilities: - Extract text from PDFs - Handle OCR fallback - Clean text - Generate metadata - Preserve page-level structure Important Insight: PDFs do not store text in human reading order. They store coordinates and drawing instructions

. import fitz import pytesseract from PIL import Image import io import os pytesseract.pytesseract.tesseract_cmd = ( r"C:\Program Files\Tesseract-OCR\tesseract.exe" ) class PDFParser: def __init__(self, file_path): self.file_path = file_path def extract_text_from_page(self, page): blocks = page.get_text("blocks") blocks.sort(key=lambda b: (b[1], b[0])) text = "" for block in blocks: block_text = block[4] text += block_text + "\n" return text.strip() def perform_ocr_on_page(self, page): pix =

= block[4] text += block_text + "\n" return text.strip() def perform_ocr_on_page(self, page): pix = page.get_pixmap() image_bytes = pix.tobytes("png") image = Image.open(io.BytesIO(image_bytes)) ocr_text = pytesseract.image_to_string(image) return ocr_text.strip() def clean_text(self, text): text = text.replace("\n", " ") text = " ".join(text.split()) return text def parse(self): doc = fitz.open(self.file_path) parsed_pages = [] for page_number in range(len(doc)): page =
doc = fitz.open(self.file_path) parsed_pages = [] for page_number in range(len(doc)): page = doc.load_page(page_number) text = self.extract_text_from_page(page) if len(text.strip()) < 20:

text = self.perform_ocr_on_page(page) cleaned_text = self.clean_text(text) parsed_pages.append({ "page": page_number + 1, "content": cleaned_text, "metadata": { "source": os.path.basename(self.file_path), "page_number": page_number + 1 } }) return parsed_pages 8. Why OCR is Needed Scanned PDFs are basically images. Normal text extraction fails on scanned pages. OCR (Optical Character Recognition) converts images into text

. OCR (Optical Character Recognition) converts images into text. OCR pipeline: Scanned PDF -> Image -> OCR -> Extracted Text Production OCR tools: - Tesseract - PaddleOCR - AWS Textract - Google Vision 9. Block-Based Parsing Using: page.get_text("text") can produce broken reading order. Better approach: page.get_text("blocks") This gives coordinate-based text blocks. Sorting blocks by: (y-position, x-position) reconstructs human reading order much better. 10
. Sorting blocks by: (y-position, x-position) reconstructs human reading order much better. 10. Metadata Management Metadata is extremely important in RAG systems.

Example metadata: { "subject": "DBMS", "chapter": "Normalization", "page": 42 } Metadata helps: - source citations - retrieval filtering - chapter-based search - PDF viewer highlighting 11. Storage Consistency You learned an important backend engineering concept: Storage consistency. Problem: documents.json may contain metadata even when actual files are deleted. Solution: - verify file existence - auto cleanup stale metadata - always delete through APIs This prevents orphaned metadata. 12

. 12. Delete API Concept Production systems never delete files manually. Instead: DELETE API removes: - PDF - metadata - parsed JSON - embeddings - vector index This keeps the system synchronized. 13. Why Parsing Matters in RAG

The intelligence of RAG systems is not only the LLM. The real quality depends on: 1. Parsing 2. Chunking 3. Retrieval Bad parsing leads to: - poor retrieval - broken chunks - hallucinations - missing context 14. Future Improvements Next modules to build: 1. chunker.py 2. embedding_service.py 3. vector_store.py 4. retrieval pipeline 5. reranking 6. semantic search Advanced parsing later: - LlamaParse - Unstructured.io - LayoutLM - formula extraction - table extraction 15

. Final Summary Congratulations. You have now built the foundation of a production-style document ingestion system. Current capabilities: - Upload PDFs - Parse PDFs - OCR scanned pages - Extract structured text - Manage metadata - View documents - Maintain storage consistency Next major step: Chunking + Embedding pipeline.
